claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To get started, I'll need to gather some information about your circumstances and your children. Let me begin with the basics:

**How many children are you looking to claim Child Benefit for?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm looking to claim Child Benefit for my child. I have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you're enquiring about Child Benefit for Alex, who is 8 years old.

Before I can assess your eligibility, I need to understand your living situation a bit better:

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. That's helpful to know.

Now I need to check a few more details about your circumstances:

**Are you a British citizen, or do you have settled status or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm a British citizen and I live here in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for confirming that.

I just need to check one more important detail:

**Are you currently receiving any income-related benefits, such as Income Support, income-based Jobseeker's Allowance, income-related Employment and Support Allowance, or Pension Credit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not receiving any of those benefits at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Let me just confirm one final detail:

**Are there any special circumstances with Alex, such as being in hospital, in local authority care, or living away from home for any reason?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, there are no special circumstances. Alex lives at home with me full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! I have all the information I need. Let me review the eligibility criteria to make my assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Based on the information you've provided and the eligibility criteria, I can now make my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Great news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the key requirements:
- Alex is 8 years old (under 16)
- Alex lives with you full-time
- You are a British citizen living in the UK
- You are responsible for Alex
- There are no special circumstances affecting eligibility
- You're not receiving income-related benefits that would impact your claim

You should be able to proceed with claiming Child Benefit for Alex. If you have any further questions about your claim, you can contact HMRC directly.

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit. The following criteria are met: (1) Alex is under 16 years old (age 8), (2) You are responsible for Alex as you live with them, (3) You live in the UK, (4) You are a British citizen, (5) Alex lives with you full-time with no special circumstances such as hospital or local authority care, (6) You are not currently receiving income-related benefits that would affect eligibility.
arguments.overall_reasoning:
You meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for Alex. As a British citizen living in the UK, responsible for an 8-year-old child who lives with you full-time, with no complicating factors, you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex."}
</invoke>
